get.description: List all domains for the organization.

schema.title: Response List Domains Organizations Domains Get

post.description:
Create a new domain for verification.

This endpoint initiates the domain verification process by:
1. Creating a domain record
2. Generating a unique verification token
3. Returning DNS configuration instructions

The user must add a DNS TXT record to verify ownership.

Verify domain ownership via DNS TXT record.

This endpoint checks for the presence of the verification TXT record
and marks the domain as verified if found.

Refresh the verification token for an unverified domain.

Generates a new token and resets the 48-hour expiry window.
This is useful when the original token has expired.

Reset a verified domain to unverified state for re-verification.

Generates a new token and marks the domain as unverified.
This allows re-verification of already verified domains.

get.description: List all SSO providers for the organization.

schema.title: Response List Providers Organizations Providers Get

post.description:
Create a new SSO provider configuration.

Supported provider types:
- oidc: OpenID Connect
- saml: SAML 2.0 (coming soon)

Test SSO provider connection.

This endpoint tests the OIDC provider configuration by fetching the
discovery document and validating required endpoints exist.
If successful, marks the provider as valid (is_valid=true).
If failed, marks as invalid and deactivates (is_valid=false, is_active=false).

get.description: Return the details of the organization.

post.description: Transfer organization ownership to another member.

get.description:
Returns a list of organizations associated with the user's session.

Returns:
    list[Organization]: A list of organizations associated with the user's session.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If there is an error retrieving the organizations from the database.

post.description: Create a new organization.

get.description: List all domains for an organization.

post.description: Add a new domain to an organization.

get.description: Get a single domain by ID.

delete.description: Delete a domain from an organization.

post.description: Verify a domain (marks it as verified).

get.description: List all SSO providers for an organization.

post.description: Add a new SSO provider to an organization.

get.description: Get a single SSO provider by ID.

delete.description: Delete an SSO provider from an organization.

get.description:
Get all workspace permissions.

Returns a list of all available workspace permissions.

Returns:
    List[Permission]: A list of Permission objects representing the available workspace permissions.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If there is an error retrieving the workspace permissions.

post.description:
Assigns a role to a user in a workspace.

Args:
    payload (UserRole): The payload containing the organization id, user email, and role to assign.
    workspace_id (str): The ID of the workspace.
    request (Request): The FastAPI request object.

Returns:
    bool: True if the role was successfully assigned, False otherwise.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the user does not have permission to perform this action.
    HTTPException: If there is an error assigning the role to the user.

delete.description:
Delete a role assignment from a user in a workspace.

Args:
    workspace_id (str): The ID of the workspace.
    email (str): The email of the user to remove the role from.
    organization_id (str): The ID of the organization.
    role (str): The role to remove from the user.
    request (Request): The FastAPI request object.

Returns:
    bool: True if the role assignment was successfully deleted.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If there is an error in the request or the user does not have permission to perform the action.
    HTTPException: If there is an error in updating the user's roles.

parameters.name: include_testcases

schema.description: Include full testcase objects. Default (null/true): include testcases. False: return only testcase IDs.
schema.title: Include Testcases

post.description:
Resolve embedded references in a workflow revision configuration.

This endpoint:
1. Fetches the workflow revision
2. Resolves all @ag.references tokens in the configuration
3. Returns the revision with resolved configuration + metadata

get.description:
Returns the list of built-in evaluator templates.

These are static evaluator type definitions (not user-created configs).

post.description:
Resolve embedded references in an environment revision configuration.

This endpoint:
1. Fetches the environment revision
2. Resolves all @ag.references tokens in the configuration
3. Returns the revision with resolved configuration + metadata
Query connections with optional filtering.
Create a new tool connection.
Refresh a connection's credentials.
Mark a connection invalid locally (does not revoke at the provider).
Call a tool action with a connection.

delete.summary: Delete simple accounts by user ref

200.description: Partial transfer — some orgs could not be transferred.

get.description:
List all API keys associated with the authenticated user.

Args:
    request (Request): The incoming request object.

Returns:
    List[ListAPIKeysResponse]: A list of API Keys associated with the user.

post.description:
Creates an API key for a user.

Args:
    request (Request): The request object containing the user ID in the request state.

Returns:
    str: The created API key.

delete.description:
Delete an API key with the given key prefix for the authenticated user.

Args:
    key_prefix (str): The prefix of the API key to be deleted.
    request (Request): The incoming request object.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing a success message upon successful deletion.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the API key is not found or does not belong to the user.

post.description:
Assigns a role to a user in an organization.

Args:
    organization_id (str): The ID of the organization.
    payload (InviteRequest): The payload containing the organization id, user email, and role to assign.
    workspace_id (str): The ID of the workspace.

Returns:
    bool: True if the role was successfully assigned, False otherwise.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the user does not have permission to perform this action.
    HTTPException: If there is an error assigning the role to the user.

Resend an invitation to a user to an Organization.

Raises:
    HTTPException: _description_; status_code: 500
    HTTPException: Invitation not found or has expired; status_code: 400
    HTTPException: You already belong to this organization; status_code: 400

Returns:
    JSONResponse: Resent invitation to user; status_code: 200

Accept an invitation to an organization.

Raises:
    HTTPException: _description_; status_code: 500
    HTTPException: Invitation not found or has expired; status_code: 400
    HTTPException: You already belong to this organization; status_code: 400

Returns:
    JSONResponse: Accepted invitation to workspace; status_code: 200

get.description:
Get workspace details.

Returns details about the workspace associated with the user's session.

Returns:
    Workspace: The details of the workspace.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the user does not have permission to perform this action.

Get all workspace roles.

Returns a list of all available workspace roles.

Returns:
    List[WorkspaceRoleResponse]: A list of WorkspaceRole objects representing the available workspace roles.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If an error occurs while retrieving the workspace roles.

delete.description:
Remove a user from a workspace.

Args:
    email (str): The email address of the user to be removed
    workspace_id (str): The ID of the workspace.

AdminAccountReadDTO.title: AdminAccountReadDTO
AdminAccountReadDTO.description: Per-account projection in the full graph response (plural entity maps).

AdminAccountsResponseDTO.title: AdminAccountsResponseDTO
AdminAccountsResponseDTO.description: Response for the full graph endpoint (POST /accounts/).

AdminSimpleAccountCreateDTO.title: AdminSimpleAccountCreateDTO
AdminSimpleAccountCreateDTO.description: One account entry in a batch simple-accounts create request.

AdminSimpleAccountDeleteEntryDTO.title: AdminSimpleAccountDeleteEntryDTO
AdminSimpleAccountDeleteEntryDTO.description:
One account entry in a batch simple-accounts delete request.

Identifies the account by its user (typically by id).

AdminSimpleAccountReadDTO.title: AdminSimpleAccountReadDTO
AdminSimpleAccountReadDTO.description:
Per-account entry in the simple-accounts response.

``user`` is a flat object (there is always exactly one per account).
``organizations``, ``workspaces``, ``projects`` are named dicts (keys match
the ref keys used internally, e.g. "org", "wrk", "prj").
``api_keys`` maps ref names to raw key values (plain strings, not DTOs).

AdminSimpleAccountsCreateDTO.title: AdminSimpleAccountsCreateDTO
AdminSimpleAccountsCreateDTO.description:
Batch simple-accounts create.

``accounts`` is a caller-keyed map of account entries.  Each entry
scaffolds one user with their own org → workspace → project graph.
Per-account ``options`` override the top-level ``options`` defaults.

AdminSimpleAccountsDeleteDTO.title: AdminSimpleAccountsDeleteDTO
AdminSimpleAccountsDeleteDTO.description:
Batch simple-accounts delete.

``accounts`` is a caller-keyed map of entries to delete.
Each entry identifies the account by its user ref.

AdminSimpleAccountsOrganizationsTransfer.title: AdminSimpleAccountsOrganizationsTransferOwnershipDTO
AdminSimpleAccountsOrganizationsTransfer.description:
Transfer ownership of one or more organizations from one user to another.

``organizations`` is a keyed map of org refs; omit to transfer all orgs owned
by the source user.
``users`` is a two-key dict: "source" (current owner) and "target" (new owner).
Both ``include_workspaces`` and ``include_projects`` default to "all".

AdminSimpleAccountsOrganizationsTransfer.title: AdminSimpleAccountsOrganizationsTransferOwnershipResponseDTO
AdminSimpleAccountsOrganizationsTransfer.description:
Response for the transfer-ownership endpoint.

``transferred`` lists the IDs of organizations whose ownership was updated.
``errors`` lists any org refs that were requested but not transferred
(e.g. not owned by the source user).

AdminSimpleAccountsResponseDTO.title: AdminSimpleAccountsResponseDTO
AdminSimpleAccountsResponseDTO.description:
Response for the simple batch endpoint (POST /simple/accounts/).

``accounts`` is keyed by the same refs used in the request so callers
can look up each created account directly.

AdminSimpleAccountsUsersResetPasswordDTO.title: AdminSimpleAccountsUsersResetPasswordDTO
AdminSimpleAccountsUsersResetPasswordDTO.description:
Patch the password on one or more existing identities.

Each identity is matched by method + subject (or email); only the
password field is updated.  No user ID in the path — the subject is
the lookup key.  Pass multiple entries to reset several identities
in one call.

ApplicationArtifactFlags.title: ApplicationArtifactFlags
ApplicationArtifactFlags.description: Application flags - is_application=True; other booleans use their normal defaults unless explicitly set.

ApplicationArtifactQueryFlags.title: ApplicationArtifactQueryFlags
ApplicationArtifactQueryFlags.description: Application query flags - filter for is_application=True.

EntityRef.title: EntityRef
EntityRef.description:
Polymorphic reference that can point to a request-local key, an
existing persisted ID, a stable slug, or an email address.
Exactly one field must be set.

EnvironmentRevisionData.title: EnvironmentRevisionData

EnvironmentRevisionDelta.title: EnvironmentRevisionDelta
EnvironmentRevisionDelta.description:
Delta operations on environment revision references.

- ``set``: references to add or update (key → dict of entity → Reference).
- ``remove``: reference keys to remove.

EvaluatorTemplate.title: EvaluatorTemplate
EvaluatorTemplate.description: Static evaluator template definition (built-in evaluator types).

OrganizationDomainCreate.title: OrganizationDomainCreate
OrganizationDomainCreate.description: Request model for creating a domain.

OrganizationDomainVerify.title: OrganizationDomainVerify
OrganizationDomainVerify.description: Request model for verifying a domain.

OrganizationProviderCreate.title: OrganizationProviderCreate
OrganizationProviderCreate.description: Request model for creating an SSO provider.

OrganizationProviderResponse.title: OrganizationProviderResponse
OrganizationProviderResponse.description: Response model for an SSO provider.

OrganizationProviderUpdate.title: OrganizationProviderUpdate
OrganizationProviderUpdate.description: Request model for updating an SSO provider.

TestsetFlags.title: TestsetFlags
TestsetFlags.description:
Placeholder for testset-level flags.

This model is intentionally empty but kept as a dedicated type so that:
- existing references to `flags: Optional[TestsetFlags]` remain valid, and
- structured flags can be added here in the future without breaking the
  surrounding DTOs.

TestsetRevisionDelta.title: TestsetRevisionDelta
TestsetRevisionDelta.description: Operations to apply to a testset revision.

TestsetRevisionDeltaColumns.title: TestsetRevisionDeltaColumns
TestsetRevisionDeltaColumns.description: Column-level operations applied to ALL testcases in the revision.

TestsetRevisionDeltaRows.title: TestsetRevisionDeltaRows
TestsetRevisionDeltaRows.description: Row-level operations applied to testcases in the revision.

ToolCall.title: ToolCall
ToolCall.description: Request envelope — wraps the raw OpenAI tool call.

ToolCallData.title: ToolCallData
ToolCallData.description: OpenAI tool_calls array item — passed verbatim from the LLM.

ToolResult.title: ToolResult
ToolResult.description: Response envelope with Agenta identity, status, and the OpenAI tool message.

ToolResultData.title: ToolResultData
ToolResultData.description: OpenAI tool message — passed verbatim back to the LLM.

WebhookEventType.title: WebhookEventType
WebhookEventType.description:
Subscribable event types — a strict subset of EventType.

Values are derived from EventType so the strings stay in sync.
To add a new subscribable event type, it must first exist in EventType.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: API server liveness and readiness status.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: Manage organizations, workspaces, SSO domains, and identity providers.

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description: Manage workspaces within an organization and their members.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Manage projects within a workspace.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User profile and account management — view profile, update username, reset password.

tags.name: Keys
tags.description: Create and revoke API keys used to authenticate programmatic requests.

tags.name: Workflows
tags.description: Workflow definitions — the runnable pipelines that back an application.

tags.name: Applications
tags.description: LLM applications — create, update, list, and delete apps.

tags.name: Evaluators
tags.description: Evaluator definitions — the metrics and judges used in evaluation runs.

tags.name: Testsets
tags.description: Test datasets — collections of input/output pairs used in evaluations.

tags.name: Testcases
tags.description: Individual test cases within a testset.

tags.name: Queries
tags.description: Saved query definitions used to filter and retrieve trace data.

tags.name: Traces
tags.description: Ingest and query traces, spans, and metrics from running applications.

tags.name: Evaluations
tags.description: Evaluation runs — execute evaluators against variants and testsets.

tags.name: Environments
tags.description: Deployment environments (e.g. production, staging) and their active variants.

tags.name: Secrets
tags.description: Manage provider credentials and secret values stored in the vault.

tags.name: Tools
tags.description: External tool connections and OAuth integrations available to applications.

tags.name: Folders
tags.description: Organize applications and other resources into folder hierarchies.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Structured event ingestion for analytics and audit purposes.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Register and manage webhooks that fire on platform events.

tags.name: OpenTelemetry
tags.description: OTLP-compatible endpoints for ingesting traces directly from OpenTelemetry-instrumented services.

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Subscription management, plans, usage, and Stripe billing (EE only).

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Internal administration endpoints — restricted to platform operators.

tags.name: Deprecated
tags.description: Legacy endpoints kept for backwards compatibility — avoid in new integrations.
